commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a 16 MiB inbound WebSocket maxPayload guard and loopback regression test for Codex Supervisor, and also modifies Azure/OpenAI Responses guarded-fetch transport code and tests.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +206. Total +247 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main constructs Codex Supervisor WebSocket clients without maxPayload, and ws 8.21.0 defaults clients to a 100 MiB inbound message limit before this PR's lower cap.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the cap compatibility choice and contributor real-behavior proof; no repair lane can provide the contributor's setup proof.

target.title: fix(codex-supervisor): bound inbound websocket frame size at 16 MiB
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.ts:288-293` constructs the inbound `WebSocket` for the Codex app-server connection without a `maxPayload` option. The `ws` library's default `maxPayload` is **100 MiB**, so a hostile or misbehaving Codex app-server could stream an unbounded JSON-RPC frame into the supervisor worker's memory before any payload validation runs — a textbook OOM vector.

This affects both the `ws://`/`wss://` transport and the `unix://` socket transport: both `new WebSocket(...)` call sites are unbounded.

## Why This Change Was Made

The `ws` library supports a per-connection `maxPayload` option that:
1. Rejects inbound frames exceeding the cap with `RangeError: Max payload size exceeded`
2. Closes the offending socket immediately
3. Emits an `'error'` event that `WebSocketCodexJsonRpcConnection` already routes through its existing `fail()` / `closedError` plumbing

The fix mirrors the pattern from `extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.ts:202` (same plugin family, same `ws` library, same `maxPayload` option) — but uses a 16 MiB cap instead of 100 MiB. 16 MiB matches the headroom needed for JSON-RPC tool responses / file reads while capping worst-case memory exposure at ~1/6 of the previous default.

The `MAX_CODEX_SUPERVISOR_WS_INBOUND_BYTES` constant is module-local rather than SDK-promoted because:
- No other plugin currently needs the same cap (verified via `grep -rn "maxPayload" extensions/` — each surface picks its own value based on frame semantics)
- The OpenClaw PR template favors minimal change scope; promoting a constant adds docs/API surface budget cost

## Evidence

```
$ grep -n "maxPayload" extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.ts
12: const MAX_CODEX_SUPERVISOR_WS_INBOUND_BYTES = 16 * 1024 * 1024;
69:           maxPayload: MAX_CODEX_SUPERVISOR_WS_INBOUND_BYTES,
72:           maxPayload: MAX_CODEX_SUPERVISOR_WS_INBOUND_BYTES,

$ grep -rn "maxPayload" extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.ts extensions/voice-call/src/media-stream.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts
extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.ts:202:    maxPayload: CODEX_SANDBOX_EXEC_SERVER_MAX_INBOUND_MESSAGE_BYTES,
extensions/voice-call/src/media-stream.ts:177:        maxPayload: MAX_INBOUND_MESSAGE_BYTES,
extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts:380:      maxPayload: MAX_REALTIME_MESSAGE_BYTES,
```

Siblings (sandbox-exec 100 MiB, voice-call media-stream 64 KiB, voice-call webhook 256 KiB) each pick a cap based on frame semantics. JSON-RPC control messages need a generous cap for tool/file responses; 16 MiB is the smallest value that fits the largest typical Codex app-server payload (large `tool/read` results) while bounding worst-case OOM well below the 100 MiB default.

## After-fix Proof

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`

```
 ✓ |extensions| extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts > connectCodexAppServerEndpoint websocket maxPayload guard > rejects an inbound frame that exceeds the 16 MiB cap 283ms
 ✓ |extensions| extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts > connectCodexAppServerEndpoint websocket maxPayload guard > accepts a frame well under the 16 MiB cap (regression: the guard does not block normal traffic) 7ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Duration  1.08s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.39s
```

Both tests use a real loopback `WebSocketServer` (`ws://localhost:<random port>`), not mocks:
- **Test 1 (rejects oversized)**: server sends a 32 MiB frame (2× the cap). Production `connectCodexAppServerEndpoint` rejects with a `RangeError: Max payload size exceeded`-flavored error from the `ws` library. The test asserts the error message matches `/max.*payload|payload.*size|too large|exceeds/i`.
- **Test 2 (regression: accepts normal)**: server sends a valid 120-byte `initialize` response. Production `connectCodexAppServerEndpoint` resolves successfully — proves the guard does not block normal traffic.

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.ts extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts` → EXIT=0
- `git diff --check openclaw/main...HEAD` → clean

## Revert-line gate (proves the regression catch is wired to the production line)

Temporarily reverting the `maxPayload` options via `git stash push extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.ts`:

```
 FAIL  |extensions| extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts > connectCodexAppServerEndpoint websocket maxPayload guard > rejects an inbound frame that exceeds the 16 MiB cap
   expect((captured as Error).message.toLowerCase()).toMatch(
                                                         ^
     /max.*payload|payload.*size|too large|exceeds/i,
   )

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 1 passed (2)
```

Without the `maxPayload` option, the `ws` library accepts the 32 MiB frame (under its 100 MiB default), the client's pending `initialize` request hangs until the 60-second timeout, and the test fails because no maxPayload-flavored error is ever produced. This proves the regression gate is wired to the production constructor — not a coincidence of mock shape. Stash restored before commit.

## Diff scope

```
 extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.ts              |  9 ++++++++-
 extensions/codex-supervisor/src/json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts | 122 +++++++++++++++++++++ (new)
 2 files changed, 130 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

- Source: 4-line constant + 4-line comment + 2 maxPayload options = 10 net insertions in 1 file
- Tests: 122 net insertions in 1 new test file (real loopback `WebSocketServer` + 2 tests, 333ms + 7ms runtime)
- Total: 130 insertions / 1 deletion across 2 files
- No committed repro script (per SKILL.md rule); the proof output above was generated inline and is not part of the diff

## Security & Privacy

Inbound frame size is bounded at the protocol layer before any payload validation runs. A hostile app-server sending an oversized frame is rejected at the `ws` library layer with an immediate socket close; the supervisor worker never allocates memory proportional to the hostile payload. Existing message-level validation (`JSON.parse` → `handleMessage`) is unchanged.

## Compatibility

- `ws` is already a dependency of `@openclaw/codex-supervisor`; no new dependency added
- The `maxPayload` option is supported by the `ws` library since v0.5.0; current version (per `package.json`) is well past that
- The 16 MiB cap is generous enough for typical JSON-RPC tool responses and file reads; legitimate app-server clients should not be affected
- No existing tests change behavior; the new `*.maxpayload.test.ts` only covers the new option

## What was not tested

Live Codex app-server connection with a real 32 MiB hostile frame from a hostile upstream. The fix uses the `ws` library's well-tested `maxPayload` enforcement; the new test exercises the same code path through a real loopback WebSocketServer. Integration coverage at this layer (vs. our test) would be the actual hostile-app-server scenario, which is out of scope for an OpenClaw PR.

## Related

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/sandbox-exec-server.ts:202` — same `ws` library, same `maxPayload` option, different surface (server-side vs. our client-side)
- `extensions/voice-call/src/media-stream.ts:177` — same pattern, different cap (64 KiB) for tighter voice frame bounds
- `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts:380` — same pattern, 256 KiB cap for voice-call webhook frames
- `extensions/voice-call/src/media-stream.test.ts:985` — sibling test "rejects oversized pre-start frames at the websocket maxPayload guard before validation runs" that inspired this test pattern (server-side; ours is client-side)

## Diff stat
- Source: 9 net insertions in `json-rpc-client.ts` (1 import section + 1 constant + 1 comment + 2 maxPayload options − 1 net from reformatting the unix/non-unix branches)
- Tests: 122 net insertions in `json-rpc-client.maxpayload.test.ts` (new file, 1 describe block, 2 it blocks)
- Total: 130 insertions / 1 deletion across 2 files
- 2 new tests added (1 hostile-frame reject + 1 normal-frame regression)
